data.title: Bridge Investment jumps on report of Apollo $1.5B takeover
data.description:
Bridge Investment Group (NYSE:BRDG) soared 12% in premarket trading after a report that Apollo Global (NYSE:APO) is acquiring the real estate investment company for about $1.5 billion in stock. Its shares have since been halted due to news being pending.

As part of the deal, Bridge Executive Chairman Bob Morse will become an Apollo partner and head of its real-estate equity business, according to a WSJ report on Monday, which cited people familiar with the matter.

Bridge has a market cap of about $960 million.

Developing story ...

data.title: Equitable seeks to boost stake in AllanceBernstein after RGA reinsurance deal
data.description:
Equitable Holdings (NYSE:EQH) has entered a reinsurance deal with Reinsurance Group of America (NYSE:RGA) that will provide Equitable with capital to boost its stake in the operating partnership of AllianceBernstein Holding (NYSE:AB) to as much as 77.5% of AB from its current 61.9% stake and to buy back about $500M of EQH shares, the company said Monday.

AllianceBernstein Holding (NYSE:AB) stock gained 4.8%, while Equitable (NYSE:EQH) stock slid 4.8% in Monday premarket trading. Reinsuerance Group of America (NYSE:RGA) shares increased 0.6%.

Under the reinsurance agreement, RGA (RGA) will reinsure 75% of Equitable's (EQH) in-force individual life insurance block on a pro-rata basis and will generate more than $2B of value of Equitable, which includes a positive ceding commission and release of capital, Equitable said. The deal is expected to close in mid-2025.

"The transaction enhances our focus on Retirement, Asset Management, and Wealth Management, which are high return on capital businesses with attractive growth prospects where we have a clear right to win," said Equitable Holdings (EQH) President and CEO) Mark Pearson.

Equitable (EQH) has started a cash tender offer to buy up to 46M units of limited partnership interests in Alliance Bernstein Holding LP (AB), at a price of $38.50 per unit for an aggregate purchase price of ~$1.8B, less any applicable tax withholding, representing a 7.8% premium over AB Holding's closing price on Feb. 21. The units covered by the offering represents ~41.7% of AB Holding's currently outstanding units.

The offering isn't conditioned upon the receipt of financing or any minimum number of units being tendered.

Equitable (EQH) also plans to repurchase $500M of its shares, above its 60%-70% payout ratio target, after the close of the reinsurance deal.

Equitable (EQH) estimates a GAAP net loss at the close of the reinsurance transaction and a reduction in book value, excluding accumulated other comprehensive income. After redeployment of the capital, EQH expects the deal to have limited impact on non-GAAP operating earnings and cash generation to add to non-GAAP operating EPS.

For RGA (RGA), the deal is expected to contribute ~$70M of adjusted operating income before taxes in 2025, based on a mid-year closing date. Adjusted operating income before tax is expected to rise to $160M-$170M in 2026, and to ~$200M per year over time. RGA expects to finance the transaction from its excess capital and proceeds from a potential debt financing, subject to market conditions.

data.title: The Fresh Factory announces $3 million private placement
data.description:
• The Fresh Factory (TSXV:FRSH:CA) on Monday said it intends to complete a non-brokered private placement of up to 33,333 proportionate voting shares at $90 voting share for total gross proceeds of up to approximately $3 million.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds from the offering to fund the growth and development of its business, including upgrades to its existing facility, and for general corporate and working capital purposes.
  • The company said the closing of the offering is expected to occur on or about March 10, 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Sigma Lithium says on track to double production capacity in 2025
data.description:
Sigma Lithium (NASDAQ:SGML) said Monday it is on track to double its production capacity in 2025, with commissioning expected to begin in Q4, after completing the foundation earthworks for the second Greentech industrial plant, staying on schedule and within budget.

The first cement has been poured, and construction has advanced to civil works, including the completion of water drainage infrastructure for the second industrial site, the company said.

Sigma Lithium (NASDAQ:SGML) guided for FY 2025 production volume of 270K metric tons from plant 1 and 30K tons from plant 2; for FY 2026, it anticipated 270K tons from plant 1 and 250K tons from plant 2.

The company said it has secured a US$100M development bank credit line from BNDES to fully fund the construction, and has decided to continue advancing the project, despite the current lithium cycle, due to its "low capital expenditure intensity."

data.title: Tantalus Systems names Christopher Allen as COO
data.description:
• Tantalus Systems (OTCPK:TNTLF) said it has appointed Christopher Allen as its COO and executive vice president of Solution Strategy, effective February 24.
  • Allen has over three decades of experience, and before joining the company, he worked with Copperleaf Technologies for a decade.

data.title: Mike Doogue appointed Allegro MicroSystems' new CEO, succeeding Vineet Nargolwala
data.description:
• Allegro MicroSystems (NASDAQ:ALGM) Monday named Mike Doogue as President and Chief Executive Officer and as a member of the Board, succeeding Vineet Nargolwala.
  • Doogue’s ascension to CEO comes after 27 years of rising through the leadership ranks at Allegro (NASDAQ:ALGM), during which time he enabled many of Allegro’s disruptive technologies, originally as an engineer and later as a business leader.
  • Prior to this promotion, Doogue served as Allegro’s Executive Vice President and its first Chief Technology Officer.
